top_categories.workout_context: Race-pace efforts that mimic London to Brighton Bike Ride's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
54 miles on paved English country roads from London to the burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
When someone attacks on paved English country roads from London to the, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
London to Brighton Bike Ride's paved English country roads from London to the forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 54 miles.
London to Brighton Bike Ride's paved English country roads from London to the rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what London to Brighton Bike Ride's paved English country roads from London to the actually does to you.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of London to Brighton Bike Ride. Train it until it feels boring.
2-minute all-out efforts on paved English country roads from London to the. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
Base fitness for London to Brighton Bike Ride. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
At London to Brighton Bike Ride, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At London to Brighton Bike Ride, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for London to Brighton Bike Ride’s 54 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Race Simulation, Durability, and VO2max to prepare you for 54 miles of paved english country roads from london to the south coast in London to Brighton, England, UK.
